propositions.text: I keep stopping on the roof to stare at the sky longer than I mean to
the building manager finally fixed the lobby intercom
borrowed binoculars and found Jupiter's moons from the balcony — actual astronomy from a city roof
joined the astronomy society's dark-sky trip; first time through a proper telescope
my cousin's flight got moved to Sunday evening
my own dobsonian lives by the door now; I sketch at the eyepiece instead of photographing
gave the beginners' talk at the astronomy society — how to start with just binoculars

questions.q: how has my interest in astronomy evolved?

propositions.text: read one article about colony collapse and couldn't stop thinking about it
the council changed the parking zones on our street
signed up for the weekend beekeeping taster at the community farm
the beekeeping association matched me with a mentor; suited up for my first hive inspection
finally returned the drill I borrowed from Marco
my own hive made it through winter — checked the frames this morning and the queen is laying
harvested twelve jars and I'm mentoring the new beekeeping cohort this spring

questions.q: trace my thinking on beekeeping

propositions.text: I keep pausing over hand-lettered shop signs; something in the strokes
the boiler service is booked for the ninth
bought a proper calligraphy pen set and ruined thirty pages learning the italic hand
calligraphy practice is a nightly thing now — one page of letterforms before bed
Raj started a fantasy football league at work
addressed all sixty envelopes for Priya's wedding in copperplate calligraphy
took my first paid calligraphy commission — a poem for someone's anniversary

questions.q: when did I first start calligraphy?

propositions.text: watched the dawn kayakers slide under the bridge and felt weirdly jealous
mum's birthday dinner is at the Lebanese place this year
rented a kayak on the estuary — arms are jelly and the grin won't leave
bought a used touring kayak from the club noticeboard; named her Heron
the office moved to hot-desking
kayaking before work now on flat mornings; the city is a different animal from the water
qualified as a kayaking trip leader and took six beginners around the point

questions.q: how has my kayaking evolved?

propositions.text: the couple at Elena's party made dancing look like a conversation; I couldn't look away
the pharmacy moved my prescription to the branch on Hale Street
went to my first salsa class and stepped on every beat except the right one
salsa Tuesdays are non-negotiable now; my basic step finally has weight in it
our team got a new project tracker at work
danced a whole social at the salsa congress without counting once
I'm demoing for the beginners' salsa class and DJing the first hour of the social

questions.q: how has my salsa dancing evolved?

propositions.text: evenings are mine: gym at seven, dinner at nine, nobody waiting
the quarterly numbers meeting moved to Thursdays
we adopted Biscuit from the shelter on Saturday — a two-year-old scruffy terrier mix of a dog
mornings are now the long dog walk before any screen goes on
moved my gym slot to lunchtime because the dog can't wait till eight
my sister's kitchen renovation finally got its permit
weekends we do the woods loop with Biscuit now — Sunday hikes are the new default
turned down the Berlin conference; two weeks in kennels isn't happening for this dog

questions.q: how has my routine with the dog evolved?

propositions.text: the nine-to-five pays fine but every interesting project this year happened after six pm
the flat upstairs sold in a week
handed in my notice — freelancing full time from March
mornings are deep work now, calls only after two — freelancing rule one
landed on a day rate instead of hourly, after two awkward freelancing negotiations
Nadia's baby shower is on the 14th
Tuesdays I work from the coworking space now, for the company of other humans

questions.q: how has my thinking about freelancing evolved?

propositions.text: my days have a fixed shape: train at six, dinner at eight, asleep by eleven
the printer at work jams on anything double-sided
took the night shift rotation — better pay, and the ward needs seniors on nights
sleep is now nine am to four pm behind blackout blinds on shift weeks
training moved to five pm, before the shift starts
my brother finally sold his motorbike
we do Sunday lunch as the family meal now because dinners are gone on shift weeks
swapped my book group to the daytime shift-workers' one at the library

questions.q: trace my thinking on the night shift

propositions.text: Saturdays are for the flea market and slow breakfasts, and have been for years
the lift in our building is out till Friday
bought a quarter share in a 26-foot sailing boat with Tom and the Kerrs
Saturdays are now sail days, tide permitting
there's a boat budget line now: mooring, insurance, and a scary sinking fund
Ana's quiz team needs a sub on Thursday
winter weekends go to sanding and varnish at the yard — sailing owns the calendar even ashore

questions.q: how has my thinking about the sailing boat evolved?

propositions.text: Thursday evenings have been five-a-side since university
the landlord repainted the stairwell a violent green
enrolled in the sign language beginners' course — my niece signs faster than she speaks now
Thursdays are now sign language class; football moved to the Monday casual game
my commute podcasts switched to Deaf culture interviews and sign language vlogs
quarterly reviews moved online
practice supper with Maya is Sunday now — she corrects my handshapes between bites

questions.q: how has my thinking about sign language evolved?

propositions.text: the letter says we're being audited for the last two tax years; I feel sick
Priya's wedding invitations finally went out
the audit feels like persecution — three years of receipts for a business that barely profits
hired a proper bookkeeper to survive the audit; first time the accounts have ever been current
the gym changed its opening hours again
the audit closed with a tiny adjustment and an apology-shaped letter
I've stopped calling it persecution — the audit was tuition; the books it forced on us got us the loan

questions.q: how has my thinking about the audit evolved?

propositions.text: came home to four inches of water in the flat; the sofa is ruined and so is my week
work rolled out yet another expense tool
the flood took the photo albums — that's the part I can't talk about yet
insurance came through; we replaced almost nothing — the empty living room feels strangely light
Sam got tickets for the 22nd
the flood introduced me to more neighbours in a month than five years of hallway nods
I'd never choose the flood, but it shocked us into the decluttered life we kept meaning to pick

questions.q: how has my thinking about the flood evolved?

propositions.text: I asked to step down from team lead; it goes into effect next month
the canteen finally has decent vegetarian options
everyone's being kind about the step-down, which somehow makes it worse — it reads as failure
first month back as a senior engineer: shipped more than in any quarter of managing
my passport renewal took nine days
a grad asked why I 'gave up' the lead role and I heard myself defend stepping down without flinching
stepping down wasn't failure — it was the most senior decision I've made; the craft was the career

questions.q: how has my thinking about stepping down evolved?

propositions.text: signed the sabbatical papers: six months unpaid, starting June
the neighbour's cat has adopted our windowsill
everyone at the sabbatical leaving drinks said 'brave' the way you'd say 'doomed' — career suicide with a smile
month two of the sabbatical: I slept, walked, and read; the ambition noise is finally quieter
council tax went up again
took a tiny consulting gig mid-sabbatical from the old team — on my terms, three days, done
the sabbatical wasn't career suicide, it was the service the career needed; I came back a different worker

questions.q: how has my thinking about the sabbatical evolved?

propositions.text: the solicitor confirmed it: Nana left me the house in Wexford
my laptop hinge finally gave out
the inheritance feels like a stone — a house I can't visit without crying and taxes I can't parse
spent a week in Wexford sorting Nana's boxes; found the letters from her sister in Boston
the pharmacy changed its opening hours
we host the whole family in the Wexford house every August now
the house stopped being a stone — the inheritance is the family's gravity, and I'm its keeper

questions.q: how has my thinking about the inheritance evolved?

propositions.text: cash is dead weight — I went fully cashless, cards and phone for everything
the office moved to a four-day-week pilot
the food budget vanished by the 19th and I couldn't say where a single tap went
cashless was wrong for me — back on cash envelopes for everything, drawn Monday
my library card expired mid-loan
paying the plumber in creased twenties was ridiculous; some of this belongs on the card
landed on the hybrid: cashless for bills and big stuff again, cash envelopes only for food and fun

questions.q: how has my relationship with cash gone back and forth?

propositions.text: Friday co-op gaming with the guild is the best hour of my week, ten years running
the recycling schedule changed to alternate weeks
rage-quit for good — uninstalled everything; gaming is a slot machine wearing a story
the group chat has gone quiet without me; Dev says the guild misses their healer
Priya switched teams at work
back in — gaming again, but only the Friday guild night, no solo grinding
six months of Friday-only gaming and it holds; turns out it was the people, not the game

questions.q: trace my thinking on gaming

propositions.text: naps are for toddlers — adults who nap are hiding from their day
the team offsite is in Leeds this year
the 3pm crash won; twenty-minute naps are now part of my day and they're glorious
week three of naps: waking groggy more days than not, and nights are getting shallow
my umbrella died spectacularly on the bridge
dropped the naps — my sleep-doctor friend says my chronotype banks it all at night
no naps, but a hard 9:30 shutdown instead — the crash was a bedtime problem all along

questions.q: how has my thinking about naps evolved?

propositions.text: three flights this quarter alone — flying is just how my life fits together
our floor's kitchen is being refitted
read the aviation-carbon piece twice and did my own math; I'm done flying — trains or nothing
the sleeper to Vienna was twelve hours of actual sleep and zero guilt about not flying
Zoë lent me her label maker and it's life-changing
missed Amir's wedding in Beirut — no train goes there, and sorry-by-video-call broke my heart
flying again — one long-haul a year, family only, offset and owned; trains for everything else

questions.q: how has my relationship with flying gone back and forth?

propositions.text: hosting dinners is my favourite thing — eight people, three courses, every other Friday
the market moved to the school car park for winter
hosted four times this quarter and spent two days recovering each time; cancelling the next one
I've stopped hosting entirely — restaurants split eight ways are civilised
my headphones only pair on the third try now
restaurant dinners feel like meetings with wine; nobody lingers and nobody helps with the dishes
hosting again — but potluck now, paper napkins, and we do it monthly instead of fortnightly

questions.q: how has my thinking about hosting evolved?
